one_line: 12th-century Tamil poet of the Chola age, composer of the Kamba Ramayanam.

appearance.age_look: Late 30s, slim but sturdy build, average height, with the wiry frame of a temple-scholarly poet used to both travel and long recitations.
appearance.face: Oval face with high cheekbones, medium-brown Tamil complexion, straight prominent nose, neatly trimmed short mustache, faint stubble on chin, a small healed scar at the left brow; default expression calm and introspective with a hint of wry confidence.
appearance.hair: Thick black hair parted at the center, pulled tightly back and gathered into a modest low knot at the nape (no topknot), with a narrow, simple cotton headband in off-white wrapped once around the head to keep hair in place.
appearance.costume: A plain but carefully draped off-white veshti (single cloth dhoti) tied at the waist with a visible front fold reaching mid-calf, a thin dark maroon border at the hem; upper body mostly bare except for a soft unbleached cotton angavastram (shawl) with a subtle thin gold stripe along the edges, worn diagonally from left shoulder under right arm and occasionally wrapped around forearms; three thin horizontal vibhuti (ash) lines on forehead and upper arms, a small red tilak at the center; simple wooden tulasi bead mala around neck; feet bare except for a single toe-ring on right second toe; minimal jewelry—a slim copper bangle on right wrist and small, plain gold ear studs.
appearance.signature: A palm-leaf manuscript bundle of the Ramayanam, bound with a thin dark cord and carried with a short, smooth wooden stylus tucked into the cord, always held close at his left side or cradled in his left arm.

ref_desc: Kambar is a medium-brown Tamil man in his late 30s with an oval face, black hair tied in a low knot with an off-white headband, wearing an off-white veshti with dark maroon border, cotton angavastram with gold stripe, simple beads and ash marks, and carrying a bound palm-leaf Ramayanam bundle with wooden stylus.
sheet_prompt: Draw a full-body anime-style model sheet of Kambar, a 12th-century Tamil poet: a medium-brown man in his late 30s with an oval face, high cheekbones, straight nose, short mustache and faint chin stubble, plus a small healed scar at his left brow, his default expression calm, reflective and slightly wry; his thick black hair is parted in the center, pulled straight back and tied into a modest low knot at the nape, held by a simple off-white cotton headband wrapped once around his head. He wears an off-white veshti (dhoti) with a clearly visible dark maroon border, wrapped to mid-calf with a prominent front fold, and a soft unbleached cotton angavastram (shawl) with a thin gold stripe along its edges draped from his left shoulder across the torso and under the right arm; his upper body is otherwise bare, showing three thin horizontal vibhuti (sacred ash) lines on his forehead and upper arms and a small red tilak at the center of his brow. Around his neck is a simple wooden tulasi bead mala, his ears have small plain gold studs, his right wrist bears a slim copper bangle, and he is barefoot with a single toe-ring on the second toe of his right foot; show his feet clearly. His signature prop is a bound palm-leaf Ramayanam manuscript, a neat stack of narrow pale leaves tied with a dark cord, with a short smooth wooden stylus tucked into the binding, cradled protectively in his left arm or held at his side. Use a restrained palette of off-white, dark maroon, warm brown, gold and black, and present him from multiple angles in the same outfit and hairstyle so his face, hair, clothing details, bead mala, ash marks and palm-leaf bundle with stylus are all clearly visible and consistent.
